title: 10 best herbal teas for heartburn
intro_desc: Herbal teas for heartburn are caffeine-free infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and spices, which can provide relief from the discomfort of heartburn. They help with heartburn by reducing inflammation, promoting digestion, and neutralizing stomach acid. Some popular herbal teas for heartburn include chamomile, ginger, marshmallow root, and licorice root teas. These teas can be enjoyed either hot or cold and make a soothing alternative to other beverages. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals can experience a significant improvement in their heartburn symptoms, ultimately enhancing their overall quality of life and enabling them to enjoy their meals without worry.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in yarrow tea work to reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can help to alleviate symptoms of heartburn such as burning sensations and discomfort. Additionally, yarrow tea has been shown to increase the production of stomach mucus, which acts as a natural barrier against acid reflux, further reducing the occurrence of heartburn episodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow tea contains flavonoids, lignans, and other polyphenols that reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.
Antioxidant activity: Yarrow's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healing.

Natural antacid action: The saponins present in yarrow tea have a natural antacid effect, helping to neutralize stomach acid and soothe heartburn symptoms by forming a protective barrier on the esophageal lining.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of yarrow have been used to treat digestive issues, including heartburn, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and ease spasms.
Bark: The bark of yarrow contains compounds that help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce acid reflux, making it effective in treating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves, approximately 1-2 teaspoons per cup.
Rinse the harvested herbs with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the cleaned herbs with boiling water in a teapot or infuser, steeping for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth, discarding the solids immediately.
Serve the yarrow tea hot, allowing it to cool slightly before consumption, and enjoy within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood teas helps with heartburn because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The tea's bitter compounds also help to stimulate digestion, preventing the backward flow of stomach acid into the esophagus and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, wormwood's antispasmodic effects help to relax the muscles in the esophageal sphincter, allowing food and digestive acids to pass more smoothly and reducing the likelihood of heartburn episodes.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood tea has antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can contribute to heartburn and acid reflux.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in wormwood tea, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, help alleviate inflammation in the digestive tract and reduce irritation caused by stomach acid.

Digestive soothing properties: Wormwood tea has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing muscle spasms and relaxing the esophageal sphincter to prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots have been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues, including heartburn, due to their bitter compounds and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Wormwood seeds contain a compound called sesquiterpene lactone, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and ease stomach discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers measuring approximately one ounce.
Combine the harvested herb with boiling water in a large saucepan to create a tea infusion mixture.
Steep the herb and water mixture for 10-15 minutes allowing the active compounds to dissolve slowly.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve discarding solids immediately.
Store the cooled wormwood tea in an airtight container refrigerating at temperatures below 40 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver birch teas helps with heartburn because its active compound, betulinic acid, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract. The tea's mucilaginous texture forms a protective barrier in the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by stomach acid reflux. Additionally, silver birch's flavonoids help strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter muscle, preventing stomach contents from flowing back up into the esophagus and alleviating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_properties: Methyl salicylate: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms.

Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant properties that can help neutralize acid reflux triggers, such as excess stomach acid, and soothe the digestive tract to alleviate discomfort associated with heartburn.
Tannins: Tannins in silver birch tea can help bind to excess stomach acid, reducing its concentration and providing relief from heartburn symptoms by neutralizing acidity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe heartburn symptoms.
Bark: Contains salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried silver birch leaves and buds from sustainable sources weighing approximately 20-30 grams.
Rinse the harvested silver birch material with cold water to remove impurities and debris gently shaking off excess.
Combine the rinsed silver birch material with one liter of boiling water allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot discarding the solids carefully.
Store the prepared herbal tea in glass bottles refrigerated at 2-4 degrees celsius for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with heartburn because of its unique composition, which includes a higher level of antioxidants and polyphenols compared to other types of tea. These compounds help soothe and protect the esophageal mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and acid reflux symptoms associated with heartburn. Additionally, white tea's gentle acidity and minimal processing also contribute to its calming effects on the digestive system, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating heartburn discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Herbal white tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the esophagus, alleviating symptoms of heartburn.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: White tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and catechins, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophageal lining and alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Tannins: Tannins, found in white tea, have astringent properties that can help reduce the production of stomach acid and soothe irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the white tea plant are used to make teas that help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
Bark: The bark of the white tea plant contains compounds that help reduce acidity in the stomach and relieve symptoms of heartburn, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh white tea leaves from camellia sinensis plants ensuring optimal flavor.
Sort and clean the harvested leaves removing any stems or damaged material immediately.
Heat filtered water to 150°f to 160°f and steep one teaspoon of dried leaves for five minutes carefully.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth retaining delicate flavors completely.
Enjoy your freshly brewed herbal white tea within an hour of preparation while still warm perfectly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cannabis teas helps with heartburn because of its unique properties that provide rapid relief from acid reflux symptoms. The cannabinoids present in cannabis, particularly CBD, interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the esophagus, allowing stomach acid to flow more smoothly back down into the stomach rather than up into the esophagus, thus alleviating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_properties: Cannabigerol (CBG): CBG has been shown to reduce inflammation and relax the lower esophageal sphincter, which can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.
Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC): THC can help reduce the perception of pain and discomfort associated with heartburn, providing relief from the burning sensation in the chest and throat.
Cannabidiol (CBD): CBD has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the esophageal mucosa, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by heartburn and acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Cannabis roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm digestive issues, making them a natural remedy for heartburn relief.
Bark: The bark of the cannabis plant contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried cannabis sativa flowers or leaves per cup of boiling water.
Combine the cannabis sativa with two cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least 10 minutes and up to 30 minutes for maximum potency.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large ceramic or glass cup.
Enjoy the tea hot, adjusting sweetness and flavor as desired, with a recommended consumption time of one hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper teas helps with heartburn because they contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm the esophagus and stomach. The capsaicin present in bell peppers has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, reducing acid reflux and alleviating symptoms of heartburn. Additionally, the natural antimicrobial properties of herbs like peppermint and chamomile in herbal blends can further aid in digestion and reduce discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_properties: Chamomile tea: Soothes the digestive tract and reduces inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
Licorice root tea: Contains compounds that may help reduce inflammation and soothe the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn and acid reflux.
Peppermint tea: May help relax the muscles in the esophagus, reducing spasms and allowing food to pass through more easily, which can provide relief from heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the esophageal lining, reducing symptoms of heartburn.
Bark: The bark of the bell pepper plant contains compounds that inhibit the production of stomach acid, providing relief from heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried bell peppers of desired color and cut them into small pieces.
Measure out two teaspoons of bell pepper pieces and add them to a pot of boiling water.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 15-20 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup or teapot.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional 5-10 minutes before serving hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Shagbark hickory teas helps with heartburn because of its unique composition, which contains a combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and tannins that work together to soothe and protect the esophageal lining. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as burning sensations and discomfort associated with heartburn. Additionally, shagbark hickory teas' natural acid-neutralizing abilities help to neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from frequent or severe heartburn episodes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Shagbark Hickory tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the esophagus, alleviating symptoms of heartburn.

Gastric-soothing compounds: The tea contains compounds like tannins, flavonoids, and saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the digestive tract, potentially reducing acid reflux and heartburn discomfort.

Carminative properties: Shagbark Hickory tea has carminative properties, meaning it can help release trapped gas and reduce bloating, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms by putting pressure on the lower esophageal sphincter.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and flavonoids, roots of shagbark hickory help to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort associated with heartburn.
Bark: The thick, rugged bark of the shagbark hickory plant is a natural demulcent, soothing and protecting the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried shagbark hickory bark, ensuring at least 2 tablespoons for each serving.
Combine the harvested shagbark hickory bark with boiling water in a heat-resistant container, allowing steeping for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, discarding solids and retaining the tea liquor.
Add honey to taste, dissolving it slowly in the warm tea using a spoon, requiring approximately 1-2 teaspoons per serving.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 3 days or freezing for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola teas helps with heartburn because it contains triterpenoid saponins that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn. Additionally, gotu kola's saponins may also increase the production of mucus, which can help to buffer stomach acid and prevent it from flowing back up into the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Gotu Kola's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as triterpenoids, can reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms.
Antacid property: The alkaline nature of Gotu Kola tea helps neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from heartburn and acidity by reducing the production of excess acid in the stomach.
Sobering agent property: Gotu Kola's saponins and flavonoids can help calm down the digestive system, reducing symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and discomfort that often accompany heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Gotu kola roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation associated with heartburn.
Bark: The bark of the gotu kola plant contains antioxidants and flavonoids, which work together to protect the esophageal lining from damage caused by stomach acid, alleviating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gotu kola leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier.
Rinse the gotu kola leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried gotu kola herb or two teaspoons of fresh gotu kola leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse and release their active compounds.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with heartburn because its soothing properties can calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to acid reflux. The flavonoids and apigenin in chamomile also help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), preventing stomach acids from flowing back up into the esophagus and causing discomfort. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory effects may help to reduce irritation and pain associated with heartburn symptoms, providing relief for those who suffer from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile contains apigenin, an antioxidant that reduces inflammation in the esophagus, which can exacerbate heartburn symptoms and discomfort.
Antacid: The flavonoids present in German chamomile have antacid properties, helping to neutralize stomach acid and alleviate the burning sensation associated with heartburn.

Soothing and calming effect: Chamomile's natural sedative properties help to relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce muscle spasms that can contribute to heartburn symptoms, promoting a sense of calm and relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe and calm digestive issues, making them effective for treating heartburn.
Flowers: The flowers contain bisabolol, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried german chamomile flowers in bulk from a reliable source weighing approximately one ounce per serving.
Measure one teaspoon of dried german chamomile flowers into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat filtered water to a temperature between 195°f and 205°f and pour it over the dried flowers allowing steeping for eight to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into another container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding the solids if desired.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving and storing any leftovers in an airtight container refrigerated for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka teas helps with heartburn because its unique blend of herbs, including ginger, chamomile, and peppermint, soothes and calms the digestive system. These natural ingredients help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn. Additionally, Eureka teas' anti-inflammatory properties can relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing pressure on the lower esophageal sphincter and preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.
remedy_properties: Alkalinity: Eureka tea has an alkaline pH level, which helps to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, alleviating symptoms of heartburn.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in Eureka tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to protect the esophageal lining from damage caused by stomach acid and reduce oxidative stress.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Certain compounds found in Eureka tea, like caffeic acid and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm the digestive system, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemons from your garden or local market weighing approximately 500 grams for the recipe.
Cut the lemons into small pieces removing seeds and membranes for efficient steeping.
Combine the lemon pieces with one quart of filtered water in a large stainless steel pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce to a simmer for 30 minutes allowing flavors to meld.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a glass container discarding solids and allowing the liquid to cool before refrigeration or serving.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat heartburn with herbal teas is by choosing teas with natural anti-inflammatory and antacid properties. Chamomile, ginger, and licorice root teas are excellent options. Chamomile can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, while ginger helps speed up digestion and neutralizes stomach acids. Licorice root tea coats the esophagus and stomach lining, protecting it from acid. Drink these teas after meals and before bedtime for optimal relief. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider if heartburn persists or worsens.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to heartburn that are treated with herbal teas include acid reflux, indigestion, and stomach cramps. Herbal teas such as chamomile, ginger, and peppermint are known for their soothing properties and can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach. Licorice root tea can also help heal the stomach lining and reduce acid production. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal teas as a treatment, especially for those with underlying medical conditions or taking medications.
